skill.name: checkpoint
skill.description: Guides agents through file-based workflow checkpoints, validation, recovery, and resume after interrupted sessions.
skill.summary: Recover interrupted AI workflows using durable checkpoint files and validated resume steps.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and filesystem patterns, but review found only Markdown examples and workflow pseudocode. No executable scripts, network activity, cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found. The skill does rely on reading and writing local checkpoint files, which is expected for its purpose.

low_findings.title: Local Checkpoint File Access
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to read, write, validate, and archive local workflow state files under memory/state and memory/reports. This is expected behavior for checkpoint recovery, but users should understand that it modifies local project state files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access behavior is directly described in the skill documentation and matches the declared Read, Write, and Glob tools. It is low risk because the paths are local workflow state paths and no exfiltration or destructive behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections occur inside Markdown code fences, inline command text, and pseudocode. I found no executable shell script, Ruby code, command substitution, or dynamic command construction in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown documentation, not runnable code. The skill metadata allows only Read, Write, and Glob, and no command execution tool is requested.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Pattern Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are caused by ordinary words and workflow file names in documentation, such as checkpoint checks, design files, and execution state. No hash function, encryption routine, port scan, host discovery, or network access pattern appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive checkpoint and workflow text, not cryptographic or reconnaissance logic. No evidence was found for malicious intent or security-sensitive implementation code.

content.value_statement: Long AI workflows can lose context after crashes, timeouts, or session restarts. This skill provides a file-based checkpoint pattern for validating state and resuming work safely.
actual_capabilities: Defines phase and execution checkpoint files for durable workflow state.
Describes how to detect interrupted workflows before starting new work.
Provides validation steps for timestamps, phase progression, output files, and incomplete writes.
Explains resume behavior for requirements, architecture, implementation, verification, and reflection phases.
Recommends atomic checkpoint writing and archiving stale or corrupted state.
limitations: Does not include executable recovery code or a packaged checkpoint library.
Requires the surrounding workflow to implement the described file operations.
Assumes checkpoint files use the documented memory directory structure.
Cannot recover missing work that was never written to disk.

use_cases.title: Resume Long Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Use checkpoint files to continue a multi-phase workflow after a session crash or timeout.

use_cases.title: Protect Multi-Step Development Tasks
use_cases.description: Record phase progress and task outputs so implementation can continue without repeating completed work.

use_cases.title: Audit Workflow Recovery Behavior
use_cases.description: Review how an agent validates old state, archives corrupted checkpoints, and asks before risky resume actions.

prompt_templates.title: Start With Checkpoints
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the checkpoint skill for this workflow. Create a phase checkpoint before starting and explain where progress will be stored.

prompt_templates.title: Resume Current Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the checkpoint skill to inspect the current workflow state, validate it, and resume from the correct phase.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Before Continuing
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether saved state is trustworthy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the checkpoint skill to validate checkpoint files, output references, timestamps, and phase progression before continuing.

prompt_templates.title: Design Recovery Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the checkpoint skill to design recovery rules for every workflow phase, including stale state, partial writes, and user approval points.

output_examples.input: A workflow was interrupted during architecture review.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the saved phase, validates available design outputs, asks for user confirmation, and resumes without rerunning completed architect work.

output_examples.input: A checkpoint file has stale timestamps and missing output references.
output_examples.output: The assistant warns that the state may be corrupted, offers to archive the old state, and starts fresh only after approval.

output_examples.input: A task output exists but the execution checkpoint still says running.
output_examples.output: The assistant reads the completed output, updates task state to match, and continues with the next pending task.

best_practices: Update checkpoint files after each phase completion, user approval, and task status change.
Validate saved state before resuming any workflow from disk.
Use atomic writes and archive old state instead of deleting it immediately.
anti_patterns: Do not trust conversation memory as the only source of workflow state.
Do not resume from stale or corrupted checkpoints without warning the user.
Do not rerun completed phases when valid output files already exist.

faq.question: What does this skill help recover?
faq.answer: It helps recover multi-phase workflows after crashes, timeouts, session exits, or interrupted user approval steps.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is Markdown guidance and only declares Read, Write, and Glob tool access.

faq.question: Where does it store checkpoint state?
faq.answer: It documents checkpoint files under memory/state and phase output files under memory/reports.

faq.question: Can it recover unsaved work?
faq.answer: No. It can only resume from state and outputs that were written to disk before the interruption.

faq.question: How does it handle corrupted state?
faq.answer: It recommends validation, user confirmation, and archiving corrupted state before starting fresh.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, and the guidance is tool-agnostic.
